mappings.rationale: Directly covers the politics of international relations including organizations, treaties, and foreign policy.
Covers collective security, arms limitation, and the prevention of war between states (subsections B.1 and B.3).
Directly covers diplomacy in negotiation of international disputes, arbitration, and mediation (subsection C.2).
Directly covers the United Nations as an international organization for maintaining peace (subsection A.2).
Directly covers peace studies including nonaggression treaties, collective security, and conflict resolution.
Covers legal frameworks governing international relations including treaties and the laws of war.
Covers terrorism as a challenge to international peace and security, and counterterrorism strategies.
Covers nuclear weapons and arms limitation treaties (subsection B.3).
Covers modern warfare including limited war, total war, guerrilla warfare, and civil wars (subsection E.1).
Directly covers civil wars and insurrections as a degree of war among states (subsection E.1).
Directly covers military strategy as an element of the conduct of war (subsection E.2.a).
Covers technological aspects of military forces and warfare.
Covers religious wars as a kind of war and the relationship between religion and armed conflict.
Covers the Cold War as a major period of international relations involving arms limitation and geopolitics.
Covers the geopolitical dimensions of international relations including territorial disputes and regional power dynamics.
Covers a worldwide war including military strategy, logistics, and its aftermath in treaties (subsections E and B.2).
Covers a total world war including military organization, strategy, and treaties terminating the war.
Directly covers a treaty terminating a major war and its consequences for international relations (subsection B.2).
Covers a regional conflict involving territorial disputes, diplomacy, and the use of military force.
Covers a civil war with international dimensions and foreign intervention (subsection E.1).
Covers a major regional and international war involving military strategy, tactics, and treaty settlements.
Covers the foundational theory of war as a continuation of politics by other means.
Covers air forces as a branch of armed forces (subsection E.3.c.iii).
Covers the historical development of organized military forces (subsection E.3.b).
Covers naval and amphibious forces as a branch of the armed forces (subsection E.3.c.ii).
Covers foreign policy and its compatibility with national and humanitarian interests (subsection C.1).
Covers crimes against peace and humanity, relevant to attempts to punish war crimes (subsection B.5).
Covers European federalism as a transnational political movement and regional integration (subsection A.1).
Covers international law relating to military discipline and the treatment of persons during wartime (subsection E.2.e).
Covers national liberation movements and the transformation of colonial international relations.
Covers religious wars as a major historical type of armed conflict (subsection E.1).
Covers the historical development of military strategy, tactics, and armed forces.
Covers territorial treaties and border disputes between states (subsection B.4).
Covers a major civil war involving military strategy, logistics, and the conduct of war.
Covers emerging powers and their role in contemporary international relations and organizations.
Covers legal protections during wartime relevant to treatment of persons (subsection E.2.e).
Covers crimes against humanity in the Soviet system, relevant to international human rights and war crimes.
Covers refugee crises resulting from wars and international conflicts.
